name: Leighton State Bank
description_short: Community bank serving customers since 1911 with retail banking, lending, and investment services across multiple locations.
description_long:
Leighton State Bank traces its origins to 1911 when it began as Farmers Savings Bank, evolving into a full-service community bank with expanded locations and service offerings. The bank maintains a strong commitment to serving as a hometown financial institution focused on helping customers achieve financial strength through personalized service.

The bank offers comprehensive banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, home lending (purchase, construction, and refinancing), commercial and agricultural lending, online and mobile banking, investment services, and business banking solutions. They provide mortgage lending through NMLS-licensed loan officers and maintain dedicated teams for retail banking, commercial lending, and residential mortgages.

Leighton State Bank distinguishes itself through active community involvement, having donated to 104 local causes in 2025 and serving as title sponsor of local events like the 410 Sprint Cars. The institution emphasizes personal relationships and tailored financial solutions, with leadership (President/CEO Steve Fopma) personally welcoming customers and highlighting flexibility in product offerings. Their "Shop Local" initiative reflects commitment to supporting local business growth.

As a traditional community bank, Leighton State Bank appeals primarily to customers seeking personalized service and local decision-making rather than large national banks. The institution's longevity and community integration are strengths, though customers should verify specific product terms, rates, and eligibility requirements typical of any financial institution. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with digital login and enrollment
Home purchase, construction, and refinance lending
Residential mortgage lending (4 NMLS-licensed loan officers)
Business banking and business relationship management
Community development and local cause donations
Personal financial planning guidance ("Tips & Tricks" blog content)
pros: 114-year operating history dating back to 1911 as Farmers Savings Bank, demonstrating stability and institutional continuity
Active community involvement with donations to 104 local causes in 2025 and title sponsorships of regional events
NMLS-licensed residential mortgage loan officers (4 on staff with specific NMLS IDs listed) providing home lending services
Multiple specialized lending teams including dedicated Ag/Commercial Lending department with 10+ relationship officers
Online and mobile banking access with downloadable apps available on both Google Play and Apple App Store
Full-service offerings spanning personal banking, business banking, lending, and investment services under one institution
Personalized relationship-based approach emphasized by leadership and staff testimonials highlighting individual customer care
cons: Limited geographic footprint as a regional/community bank compared to national banking alternatives
No specific information provided about fees, minimum account balances, interest rates, or APR terms on website
Lending products appear to require in-person relationship building; less accessible for customers preferring purely online banking
Investment services department mentioned but limited details on specific investment products, management fees, or advisor qualifications available
No disclosure of FDIC insurance coverage limits, deposit insurance details, or other regulatory protections on public website
best_for: Local residents and business owners in their service area seeking personalized banking relationships with local decision-makers
Agricultural and commercial borrowers needing specialized lending with dedicated ag/commercial lending teams
Homebuyers and refinancing customers in their region looking for mortgage services from NMLS-licensed loan officers
diagnosis: Leighton State Bank is best for customers in their geographic service area seeking relationship-based community banking with personalized service, particularly those needing agricultural, commercial, or residential lending. Primary caveat: as a regional bank, availability depends on location, and customers should directly contact them to verify specific products, rates, fees, and qualification requirements not detailed on their public website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
